parsed_review.summary: CP00-432 (settlement core, Risk C, 150 units, RP14.P02.M06.S09-RP14.P03.M04.S05) passes read-only review with one non-blocking P3. The pack correctly does NOT claim production_ready (production_ready: false; status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); the claim actually under review is descriptor-layer verification, which is fully supported by registry bindings (SETTLEMENT_CORE_CP432_PACK_BINDING + REQUIREMENTS), service descriptors (createSettlementCoreCp432P02CloseoutP03FoundationDescriptor, freezeCp432Result), validators (coverage + foundation descriptor + Hermes/Claude packet builders), tests (model.test.js CP00-432 cases), and contract consistency (contracts/settlement-core-contract.json current_pack + historical tail = CP00-432, validated by scripts/validate-rp14-settlement-core-contract.mjs). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via frozen attestations (synthetic_only, no_real_data true, writes_product_state false, dispatches_*_runtime false). Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust false — Claude stays a read-only reviewer, not final approver. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is present: forbidden_review_evidence and invalid_review_blockers reject auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; auth preflight passed with valid JSON result. All four coverage distributions match the prompt baseline (unit_count 150, RP14-only, correct first/last units, deliverable/micro_title/phase/section-row counts). No TODO/FIXME/placeholder/throw stubs in the settlement package. No P0, P1, or P2 issues found.

findings.title: Pack's own untracked closeout dir is dual-classified in scope manifest
findings.details: The pack's own untracked closeout artifacts — docs/closeout-packs/cp00-432/ (line 138) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md (line 137) — appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133, i.e. classified as part of this pack) AND unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145, i.e. classified as unrelated). This is a benign cosmetic inconsistency in the changed-file-scope manifest. It has no effect on the review outcome because manifest.json's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly EXCLUDES the pack directory, and the truly unrelated dirty files (the v2.0.docx, workbook/* matter docs, the c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt JSON) are independent of CP00-432 and were correctly not inferred as pack scope.
findings.recommendation: Optionally de-duplicate the changed-file-scope.json classification so the pack's own untracked closeout artifacts appear only under untracked_implementation_files and not also under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No action required for closeout; informational only.
findings.post_review_disposition: Accepted as non-blocking; informational only. Does not block CP00-432 pack closeout or goal closeout.
